title: Day 3: fab CLI — 5 Subcommands (init / sync-meta / human-lint / ledger-append / scan)
description: Implement all 5 fab CLI subcommands using citty. fab scan is the heuristic chain that inspects a project: parse package.json deps to detect framework (vite/next/cargo/pyproject/Cocos Creator), check README quality (stub if <200 words), output JSON diagnostic report. fab init consumes scan output and generates a non-destructive AGENTS.md scaffold with // TODO markers (Nx-style — never AI-guess content); on the werewolf-minigame-stub fixture it must only ADD files, never modify existing ones. fab sync-meta hashes the AGENTS.md tree and updates .fabric/agents.meta.json. fab human-lint and fab ledger-append are scaffolded here (fully implemented in TASK-004). DEFAULT_IGNORES for Cocos Creator projects included in scan.

files.change: Heuristic chain: detect framework from package.json/Cargo.toml/pyproject.toml/project.config.json (Cocos); README word count check; DEFAULT_IGNORES (**/*.meta, library/, temp/, build/); output JSON diagnostic report to stdout; --target and --debug flags

Consume scan JSON output; generate AGENTS.md scaffold with // TODO markers at L0 root + .fabric/agents.meta.json (L0 only) + .husky/pre-commit template; NEVER modify existing files; NEVER AI-guess content
Walk AGENTS.md tree; compute sha256 hash per file; update .fabric/agents.meta.json nodes[*].hash and top-level revision field; --check-only flag exits 1 if meta is stale without writing
Skeleton command: read .fabric/human-lock.json; compare @HUMAN section hashes against current files; exit 1 on mismatch (full logic completed in TASK-004)
Skeleton command: --staged flag; derive intent entry from git diff --cached; append to .intent-ledger.jsonl (full logic completed in TASK-004)

files.target: CLI entry point with all 5 subcommands registered
files.change: Update citty defineCommand to import and register all 5 subcommands; fab --help lists all 5
Detect vite/next.js/cargo/pyproject/Cocos (project.config.json) from project files; return FrameworkInfo object
Export DEFAULT_IGNORES array: **/*.meta, library/, temp/, build/, node_modules/, dist/, .git/; configurable extension via fabric.config.json
L0 AGENTS.md template with // TODO markers, <!-- fab:index --> placeholder, @HUMAN section skeleton, and L0 AI constraint stubs
Initial pre-commit template referencing fab subcommands; full pipeline completed in TASK-004

implementation: Implement packages/cli/src/scanner/detector.ts: check for package.json (vite/next deps), Cargo.toml, pyproject.toml, project.config.json (Cocos Creator marker); return { framework, confidence, configFile }
Implement packages/cli/src/scanner/ignores.ts: export DEFAULT_IGNORES with Cocos-specific entries (**/*.meta, library/, temp/, build/); merge with fabric.config.json ignores if present

Implement fab scan in scan.ts: accept --target <path> (default: cwd) and --debug flags; run detector chain; count README.md words (stub if <200); apply DEFAULT_IGNORES to file walk; output JSON diagnostic { framework, readme_quality, agents_md_exists, fabric_initialized, timestamp }

Implement fab init in init.ts: run scan internally or accept --from <scan-output.json>; for missing-docs projects generate L0/AGENTS.md with // TODO markers (use template); generate .fabric/agents.meta.json with only L0 node; generate .husky/pre-commit from template; NEVER call fs.writeFile on any path that already exists (check first)

Implement fab sync-meta in sync-meta.ts: walk all AGENTS.md files referenced in .fabric/agents.meta.json; compute sha256 of each file content; update nodes[path].hash; recompute top-level revision as sha256 of all node hashes concatenated; support --check-only flag

Scaffold fab human-lint in human-lint.ts: parse .fabric/human-lock.json; for each locked section compute current hash and compare; exit 1 if any mismatch with descriptive error (full implementation in TASK-004)

Scaffold fab ledger-append in ledger-append.ts: --staged flag runs git diff --cached; derive intent string from diff summary; append JSON line to .intent-ledger.jsonl (full implementation in TASK-004)

Register all 5 subcommands in packages/cli/src/index.ts; verify fab --help output lists all 5; test fab init on werewolf-minigame-stub fixture (create stub fixture in examples/ per TASK-006 plan, or use a temp minimal fixture)

reference.pattern: citty subcommand pattern; Nx-style TODO-marker scaffolding (non-destructive); convention-based framework detection
reference.examples: mcp-first-fabric.md §4.5 for fab init behavior (non-destructive, reference not merge); citty npm package for CLI framework; sha256 via built-in Node crypto module

criteria: fab --help lists exactly 5 subcommands: init, sync-meta, human-lint, ledger-append, scan
fab scan on a project with project.config.json (Cocos marker) produces JSON diagnostic report with framework:'cocos', and **/*.meta files are excluded from file counts
fab init on a project with no AGENTS.md generates AGENTS.md scaffold containing // TODO markers; .fabric/agents.meta.json created with L0 node only
fab init on werewolf-minigame-stub (or minimal fixture with existing files): zero existing files modified; only new files added (AGENTS.md, .fabric/agents.meta.json, .husky/pre-commit)
fab sync-meta: after running on a valid AGENTS.md tree, .fabric/agents.meta.json.revision updated; nodes[*].hash updated; --check-only on unmodified tree exits 0; --check-only on stale tree exits 1

rationale.chosen_approach: citty for CLI framework (lightweight, TypeScript-native); Node built-in crypto for sha256; separate scanner module for framework detection to keep command files focused
decision_factors: citty is locked in handoff spec as the CLI framework
fab init non-destructive behavior is a critical safety constraint from key_findings
Cocos Creator DEFAULT_IGNORES explicitly required in handoff spec
rationale.tradeoffs: Splitting scanner into separate module adds a file but keeps scan.ts readable and testable

manual_checks: Create a temp dir with package.json containing vite dep; run fab scan — must report framework: vite
Create a temp dir with project.config.json; run fab scan — must report framework: cocos; *.meta files excluded
Create a temp dir with existing README.md and no AGENTS.md; run fab init — AGENTS.md created with // TODO markers; README.md unmodified
Run fab sync-meta on a valid AGENTS.md tree; modify the file; run fab sync-meta --check-only — must exit 1
success_metrics: fab scan correctly identifies all 5 framework types (vite, next, cargo, pyproject, cocos)
fab init: 0 existing files modified in all test scenarios
fab sync-meta: revision hash changes on any AGENTS.md modification

risks.description: fab init fixture test requires werewolf-minigame-stub to exist (created in TASK-006); Day 3 may need a minimal ad-hoc fixture
risks.mitigation: Create a minimal temp fixture inline in the test (package.json + 1 .ts file + README.md) for Day 3 validation; proper stub created in TASK-006
risks.fallback: Use a manually created temp directory for Day 3 smoke testing

interfaces.name: ScanDiagnostic
interfaces.definition: { framework: string | null; readme_quality: 'stub' | 'adequate'; agents_md_exists: boolean; fabric_initialized: boolean; recommended_l1_candidates: string[]; timestamp: string }
interfaces.purpose: Output type for fab scan JSON report

interfaces.name: AgentsMeta
interfaces.definition: { revision: string; nodes: Record<string, NodeMeta>; human_locked: HumanLock[] }

key_functions.purpose: Heuristic chain to identify project framework from config files
Generate AGENTS.md + .fabric/agents.meta.json without touching existing files
Compute sha256 of all node hashes to produce tree revision hash
